title: Day 1: What is Automation and No-Code?
content: Overview of tools that allow you to build and automate without code.
exercise: Identify two repetitive tasks you do daily that could potentially be automated.

title: Day 2: The No-Code Ecosystem
content: Overview of Zapier, Make, Bubble, Airtable, and Webflow.
exercise: Explore the landing pages of 3 different no-code tools and note their primary use cases.

title: Day 3: Thinking in Logic (If/Then/Else)
content: The foundation of all automation.
exercise: Write down a logic flow for an automation: 'If I receive an email with an attachment, then...'

title: Day 4: Intro to Airtable
content: Beyond spreadsheets: building relational databases.
exercise: Create a base in Airtable to track your personal tasks with fields for Name, Status, and Due Date.

title: Day 5: Airtable: Linking and Relationships
content: How to connect different data sets together.
exercise: Create a second table for 'Categories' and link it to your 'Tasks' table.

title: Day 6: Intro to Zapier
content: Connecting apps together and moving data.
exercise: Set up a simple Zap: 'When a new row is added to a Google Sheet, send a Slack message'.

title: Day 7: Zapier: Multi-step Automations
content: Building complex workflows.
exercise: Create a Zap that does 3 things: Trigger -> Add Row -> Send Email -> Create Calendar Event.

title: Day 8: Intro to Make.com
content: Visual automation for more advanced users.
exercise: Re-create your simple Zapier flow in Make to see the difference in interface.

title: Day 9: Make.com: Filters and Routers
content: Controlling the flow of your automation.
exercise: Add a filter to your Make scenario so it only proceeds if a specific condition is met.

title: Day 10: APIs for No-Coders
content: Connecting to anything with a URL.
exercise: Research what an 'API Key' is and where to find one for a tool like OpenAI or Gmail.

title: Day 11: Intro to Webflow
content: Building professional websites visually.
exercise: Create a blank Webflow project and add a 'Section' and a 'Container'.

title: Day 12: Webflow: The Style Panel
content: Applying CSS visually without writing code.
exercise: Change the background color, font, and padding of a card in your project.

title: Day 13: Webflow CMS Basics
content: Powering your site with dynamic data.
exercise: Create a 'Blog Post' collection in Webflow with fields for Title, Body, and Image.

title: Day 14: Integrating Zapier with Webflow
content: Automating site updates.
exercise: Set up an automation that creates a new CMS item in Webflow when a Google Form is submitted.

title: Day 15: Intro to Bubble.io
content: Building complex web applications visually.
exercise: Explore the Bubble editor and identify where the database and workflow tabs are.

title: Day 16: Bubble: Workflows and Logic
content: Making your app 'do' things.
exercise: Create a workflow in Bubble: 'When a button is clicked, change the text on the screen'.

title: Day 17: Bubble: Data Types and Privacy
content: Managing user data securely.
exercise: Create a 'User Profile' data type in Bubble with fields for Bio and Profile Picture.

title: Day 18: No-Code Mobile Apps (Adalo/Glide)
content: Building apps directly for phone screens.
exercise: Create a simple list app in Glide from a Google Sheet.

title: Day 19: AI + Automation (No-Code AI)
content: Integrating GPT-4 into your workflows.
exercise: Create a flow that takes an email, summarizes it using AI, and sends a summary to Slack.

title: Day 20: Scraping Data without Code (Browse.ai/Apify)
content: Gathering info automatically.
exercise: Use a browser extension to 'scrape' a list of products from an e-commerce page into a CSV.

title: Day 21: Error Handling in Automation
content: What happens when things break?
exercise: Research what a 'Webhook' is and why it's faster than polling for data.

title: Day 22: Developing a 'No-Code' Solution for a Business
content: Mapping out the problem first.
exercise: Select a local business and map out how automation could save them 5 hours a week.

title: Day 23: Dashboard Design (Softr/Stacker)
content: Building portals for clients and internal teams.
exercise: Connect Softr to an Airtable base and display the data as a list of items.

title: Day 24: SEO for No-Code Sites
content: Optimizing Webflow and Bubble for search.
exercise: Check the SEO settings in a Webflow project and add global meta tags.

title: Day 25: Performance & Optimization in No-Code
content: Keeping your apps fast as they grow.
exercise: Research how to use 'Backend Workflows' in Bubble to improve app speed.

title: Day 26: Launching and Testing
content: Deploying your no-code project to the world.
exercise: Run a 'test run' of your automation with different inputs to check for edge cases.

title: Day 27: Documenting your Automation
content: So others (and future you) understand it.
exercise: Create a loom video or a document explaining how one of your workflows works.

title: Day 28: Developing your Portfolio
content: Showing the 'Under the Hood' of your solutions.
exercise: Choose your best automation project and write a brief case study on the time/money it saves.

title: Day 29: Final Project: The Automated Business Bot
content: Putting it all together.
exercise: Build a complete system where a user submits a form, AI processes it, and a customized result is emailed and stored.

title: Day 30: The Future of No-Code
content: Staying on the cutting edge of visual development.
exercise: Establish a goal to learn one advanced platform (like FlutterFlow) or a coding fundamental.
